parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: The Timer cooks: Never / Always / While Running (while in "running" state) / While Playing (while in "running" state and Play is on).

parameters.name: timecontrol
parameters.label: Time Control
parameters.description: (timeline-independent) or . In Locked to , non-deterministic features are disabled. lets you drive the master time ( etc) using a channel defined by the parameters on the External page. lets you drive

parameters.name: deferpars
parameters.label: Defer Par Changes
parameters.description: When On, parameter changes like Length, Cycles and others are ignored until the next Intitalize. When Off, paraameter changes affect the timer immediately (possibly giving jumps in state).

parameters.name: initialize
parameters.label: Initialize
parameters.description: (pulse parameter) Initialize is the signal to get the timer ready: sets the frames, samples and fraction counters to zero (delay, timer, cycle, segment), set the output channels in the proper state, t

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: (pulse) Start is the signal to begin the timers counting. It will count through the delay first, then the timer length. It does an Initialize if it is not already initialized, and then starts counting

parameters.name: lengthtype
parameters.label: Length Type
parameters.description: Describes how the length is defined.

parameters.name: length
parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: (float) the time-length of the timer. Set the Units menu to Seconds, Frames or Samples.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.label: Delay
parameters.description: (float) after Start, the delay before the timer begins counting.

parameters.name: play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: (onoff) Pauses the timer. It is basically a 0 or 1 multiplier on the Speed.

parameters.name: speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: (default 1) Slows down or speeds up the timer.

parameters.name: cue
parameters.label: Cue
parameters.description: Freezes playing at the Cue .

parameters.name: cuepoint
parameters.label: Cue Point
parameters.description: Time (Seconds, Frames or Fraction) which the cue point is frozen to.

parameters.name: cycle
parameters.label: Cycle
parameters.description: (default Off) causes the timer to loop back to 0 when it reaches the end of the cycle.

parameters.name: cyclelimit
parameters.label: Cycle Limit
parameters.description: When the Cycle parameter is On, this determines if it will cycle indefinitely or cycle some maximum number of cycles.

parameters.name: maxcycles
parameters.label: Maximum Cycles
parameters.description: When Cycle is on and Cycle Limit is on, this sets the maximum number of cycles.

parameters.name: cycleendalert
parameters.label: Cycle End Alert
parameters.description: The number of seconds, frames or samples before a cycle, segment or done state is reached that the callback is called. This allows you to prepare for the next cycle, segment or timer.

parameters.name: exitendcycle
parameters.label: Exit Segment at End of Cycle
parameters.description: When pulsed, it will exit the cycle (and segment) at the end of the currently-playing cycle.

parameters.name: gotoendcycle
parameters.label: Go to End of Cycle
parameters.description: When pulsed, it will exit the cycle (and segment) immediately.

parameters.name: gotodone
parameters.label: Go to Done
parameters.description: Will immediately go to the Done state.

parameters.name: ondone
parameters.label: On Done
parameters.description: Determines which action to take when the timer gets to the end, ie "is done" or finished. Note there is also a onDone callback that can be used for customizing behavior.

parameters.name: callbacks
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: The path to the containing callbacks for this Timer .

parameters.name: lengthunits
parameters.label: Length Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: delayunits
parameters.label: Delay Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: cuepulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: Jump instantly to the Cue .

parameters.name: cueunits
parameters.label: Cue Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, Seconds, Fraction(0-1) as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: notifyunits
parameters.label: Notify Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: segdat
parameters.label: Segments DAT
parameters.description: A table that contains one row per timer (segment). The column headings can be or , , , , and , which override the equivalent parameters. (These are the internal names for the corresponding parameters.

parameters.name: segmethod
parameters.label: Segment Method
parameters.description: If the Segment Method is , the timers will be played back-to-back. If the Segment Method is , the timers can be played at the same time, and a set of channels will be output for each timer.

parameters.name: segunits
parameters.label: Segment Units
parameters.description: For the columns , , and , you specify whether it’s seconds, frames or samples with this menu.

parameters.name: segsendtime
parameters.label: Segments End Time
parameters.description: Describes how the end time is calculated.

parameters.name: channelcolumns
parameters.label: Columns to Custom Channels
parameters.description: Optional extra columns (any name) in the segments can be output as extra channels (the columns must contain numbers). Specify their names in the Columns to Channels parameter. The channel name will be

parameters.name: interpolation
parameters.label: Custom Channel Interpolation
parameters.description: By default, custom channels step to their new value at the begin of the segment. This menu lets you interpolate to the new value linearly, or any combination of ease-in and ease-out.

parameters.name: infocolumns
parameters.label: Columns to Info DAT
parameters.description: Optional extra columns (any name) in the segments can be output to the Info (attach an Info to the Timer ) if you specify their names in this parameter.

parameters.name: gotoprevseg
parameters.label: Go to Previous Segment
parameters.description: (pulse) Jump to Previous Segment.

parameters.name: gotonextseg
parameters.label: Go to Next Segment
parameters.description: (pulse) Jump to Next Segment.

parameters.name: subrange
parameters.label: Sub Range
parameters.description: Turn this parameter on to limit the timer output to a subrange of the full length.

parameters.name: substart
parameters.label: Sub Start
parameters.description: The beginning point of the sub range.

parameters.name: subend
parameters.label: Sub End
parameters.description: The end point of the sub range.

parameters.name: subendaction
parameters.label: Sub End Action
parameters.description: Controls the behavior once the sub range end point is reached: Loop at End, or Pause at End.

parameters.name: substartunits
parameters.label: Sub Start Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: subendunits
parameters.label: Sub End Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: outfraction
parameters.label: Timer Fraction
parameters.description: Outputs channel for each segment.

parameters.name: outtimercount
parameters.label: Timer Count
parameters.description: Outputs the elapsed Seconds channel as , Frames outputs channel as , or Samples outputs channel as . Because this is elapsed time, starts at 0, as do the others.

parameters.name: outtimeractive
parameters.label: Timer Active
parameters.description: Outputs channel which is on only while the timer fraction is counting (is non-zero).

parameters.name: outtimerpulse
parameters.label: Timer Pulse
parameters.description: Outputs channel when the timer reaches its length.

parameters.name: outdelayfraction
parameters.label: Delay Fraction
parameters.description: Outputs a 0-1 fraction in while the delay occurs.

parameters.name: outdelaycount
parameters.label: Delay Count
parameters.description: Outputs the delay count in seconds, frames or samples.

parameters.name: outinit
parameters.label: Initializing
parameters.description: Outputs channel = 1 while the timer is initalizing (i.e. while the callback returns non-zero).

parameters.name: outready
parameters.label: Ready
parameters.description: Outputs channel which is 1 after an Initialize and before a Start.

parameters.name: outreadypulse
parameters.label: Ready Pulse
parameters.description: Outputs a pulse when initialization has finished and the timer is ready to start. It pulses even when the timer starts rights away after an initialization.

parameters.name: outrunning
parameters.label: Running
parameters.description: Outputs channel which is 1 after a Start and before the Done.

parameters.name: outdone
parameters.label: Done
parameters.description: Outputs channel when done or complete.

parameters.name: outdonepulse
parameters.label: Done Pulse
parameters.description: Outputs channel when the all timers have reached their completion.

parameters.name: outcycle
parameters.label: Cycles
parameters.description: Outputs channel , which is the number of cycles completed (In a segment), starting with 0 during the entire first cycle. If you jump to Done, cycle is incremented as if it played normally to the done

parameters.name: outcyclepulse
parameters.label: Cycle Pulse
parameters.description: Outputs a pulse at the end of every cycle, even on the first and only cycle.

parameters.name: outcycleplusfraction
parameters.label: Cycles + Fraction
parameters.description: Outputs channel , starting with 0 for entire first cycle.

parameters.name: outseg
parameters.label: Segment
parameters.description: Outputs channel , starting with 0 for first segment.

parameters.name: outsegpulse
parameters.label: Segment Pulse
parameters.description: Outputs channel which is a pulse at the end of each segment.

parameters.name: outsegplusfraction
parameters.label: Segment + Fraction
parameters.description: Outputs channel , starting with 0 for first segment ending at #segments at end.

parameters.name: outlength
parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: Outputs channel , starting with 0 for first segment ending at #segments at end.

parameters.name: outcumulativecount
parameters.label: Cumulative Timer Count
parameters.description: Outputs , or . It is a time count that adds up all the Timer Active times for all segments since Start: it is affected by "Speed", and counts up only while (Play) is on.   See the python member .

parameters.name: outplayingcount
parameters.label: Playing Timer Count
parameters.description: Outputs , or . It is a time count that adds up all the Timer Active times for all segments since Start: it is not affected by "Speed", and counts up only while and is on.  See the python member .

parameters.name: outrunningcount
parameters.label: Running Time Count
parameters.description: Outputs the "wall-clock" time since Start occurred, no matter what are the delays, speeds, cycles or pre-mature clicking of Go To Segment End, etc. It stops counting when Done has been reached. , , or

parameters.name: outmastercount
parameters.label: Master Time Count
parameters.description: Outputs , or . It is a time count that adds up all the Timer Active times for all segments since Start: it is affected by "Speed", and counts up only while and is on. It also includes any delay times.

parameters.name: extchop
parameters.label: External CHOP
parameters.description: The used to control the current point in the timer.

parameters.name: extunits
parameters.label: External Units
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: extchannel
parameters.label: External Channel
parameters.description: The channel that will control the current point of the timer.

parameters.name: exttcop
parameters.label: External Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: Time is specified using a timecode. Should be a reference to either a with channels 'hour', 'second', 'minute', 'frame', a with a timecode string in its first cell, or a object.

parameters.name: extstartoff
parameters.label: External Start Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the external time at which the timer should start.

parameters.name: extinitoff
parameters.label: External Initialize Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the external time at which the timer should initialize.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: The sample rate that the outputs at, which is also used when the units of Length, Delay and Cycle End Alert time are set to Samples. The default sample rate is 60 samples per second.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: runcountidx
parameters.label: Runcountidx
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

examples.label: Configure Time Slice and Export Method
examples.code:
n = op('timer1')
n.par.timeslice = True
n.par.exportmethod = 'datname'
n.par.deferpars = True
examples.description: Common settings from 54 real Derivative examples
